name: OpenGov Government Performance & Budgeting API
what_it_does: OpenGov REST API for cloud-based government budgeting, financial reporting, and performance management platform. Enables AI agents to manage government budget data retrieval and visualization for fiscal transparency, handle financial reporting and CAFR (Comprehensive Annual Financial Report) data access, access performance management KPI tracking and metric data for government accountability, retrieve operational analytics and workforce productivity data for government efficiency analysis, manage budget planning and scenario modeling data for government financial agents, handle community engagement and resident feedback data for participatory budgeting, access revenue and expenditure tracking by fund, department, and program, retrieve capital improvement plan (CIP) data for infrastructure investment tracking, manage procurement and vendor contract data for government spending transparency, and integrate government financial data with ERP, state reporting, and civic analytics platforms.
use_cases: Accessing government budget data from financial transparency agents
Tracking performance KPIs from government accountability agents
Analyzing spending by department from fiscal analysis agents
Reporting on capital projects from infrastructure management agents
not_for: Private sector financial management without government budgeting and GAAP compliance context
Federal government without state and local government focus
Real-time transaction processing without government reporting and planning context
best_when: A local or state government using OpenGov wants AI agents to automate budget analysis, financial reporting, KPI monitoring, spending transparency, and ERP integration.
avoid_when: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE RISK: Government financial automation must comply with GAAP, GASB standards, and state auditing requirements — automated journal entries and budget transfers require segregation of duties and approvals. Budget data automation that bypasses appropriation authority limits violates fiscal law.

auth.notes: OpenGov uses OAuth 2.0 authentication. Per-government agency API credentials. Developer portal at developer.opengov.com. No native webhooks — polling-based data retrieval. Integration with Tyler Munis, BS&A, and SAP for ERP data sync. Public reporting and transparency features are often unauthenticated for public access. Acquired by Cox Enterprises (2021).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public transparency data accessible without auth; full API requires government subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual government subscription; modules (budgeting, reporting, performance, procurement) separately priced
pricing.notes: Redwood City, California. Founded 2012. Private (Cox Enterprises, acquired 2021). Government financial transparency and performance market. 1,500+ government clients. Known for public-facing budget visualization. OpenGov Transparency Cloud for public data. Competes with Tyler Technologies and ClearGov for government financial transparency.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: GOVERNMENT COMPLIANCE: Budget automation must comply with GASB and state appropriation law; automated budget transfers require approval chain and segregation of duties
Public vs. authenticated data — OpenGov public transparency portal often accessible without auth; full operational data requires agency OAuth credentials
Fund accounting complexity — government fund accounting (General Fund, Capital, Enterprise) is complex; understand fund structure before building financial automation
ERP integration dependency — OpenGov financial data is often sourced from ERP (Tyler Munis, SAP); understand data flow before building automation
No webhooks — implement scheduled polling for budget and performance data updates; data refreshes follow government fiscal calendar patterns
GASB compliance — government financial reporting follows GASB standards different from GAAP; automated reporting must produce GASB-compliant output
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government budgeting and financial reporting. SOC2, GASB, GAAP. OAuth2. US. Government financial and performance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government finance. SOC2. OAuth2. US. Government budget and performance data.
